question.text: As per the SEBI circular of October 2024 (effective November 2024), what is the revised minimum contract value for equity derivative contracts?

answer.text: The contract value shall not be less than Rs. 15 lakhs at introduction, and the lot size shall be set so the contract value falls between Rs. 15-20 lakhs on review
The minimum contract value remains at Rs. 5 lakhs as originally stipulated
The minimum contract value has been raised to Rs. 25 lakhs per contract
The contract value must be exactly Rs. 10 lakhs for all derivative contracts

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Per SEBI Circular SEBI/HO/MRD/TPD-1/P/CIR/2024/132 dated October 01, 2024, the minimum contract value was raised from the earlier Rs. 5-10 lakh range to Rs. 15 lakhs at introduction. The lot size must be fixed so the contract value falls between Rs. 15-20 lakhs on review. This was effective from November 2024.
**The Trap:** The old limit of Rs. 5 lakhs was set in 2015. Since then, broad market values increased ~3x, necessitating the upward revision. Rs. 25 lakhs is too high — the ceiling is Rs. 20 lakhs, not the floor.
**Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI's minimum contract size requirement is a frequently tested regulatory fact in Series VIII and Series XIII.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Higher contract values mean larger margin requirements, effectively increasing the capital needed for retail F&O participation — a deliberate SEBI measure to protect small investors.
